impossible_combinations.anomaly: Lens model does not exist for the specified iPhone model.

impossible_combinations.context: EF lens on RF body. Check for LensID/Adapter presence.
impossible_combinations.anomaly: Possible mismatch between Lens mount and Camera body if no adapter is present in EXIF.

impossible_combinations.context: E-mount APS-C lens on a full-frame A7 series body. Possible but requires crop mode.
impossible_combinations.anomaly: Crop mode should be active. If ImageWidth/ImageHeight match full sensor resolution, metadata is likely spoofed.
Software indicates editing, not a native Samsung camera save.
Camera make is specified but MakerNotes are entirely missing. Common indicator of third-party re-saving or stripping.

Facebook.notes: Facebook aggressively strips MakerNotes and EXIF but embeds a tracking code in the IPTC metadata block.

WhatsApp.notes: WhatsApp strips all identifiable metadata. Images are heavily compressed and lack JFIF resolution units.

Telegram.notes: Strips all metadata when sent as compressed photo. No known tracking tags injected. Cannot distinguish easily from a standard image stripped by generic tools.

Instagram.notes: Instagram may leave the 'Software' tag as 'Instagram' and strips original EXIF. Image resolution is capped (e.g., 1080x1080 or 1080x1350).
